dialogueChunks.intro: The workers at {quest_origin} stopped showing up three days ago. The founding families are threatening to replace them with hired muscle. Both sides are sharpening things that shouldn't be sharpened.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The workers say they haven't been paid fairly in months. The elite say the workers are lazy and ungrateful. The truth, as usual, is that the people at the top are eating well and the people at the bottom are choosing between food and firewood. {quest_focus} has been building for years.
dialogueChunks.outro: Someone from outside needs to {quest_action} before {quest_threat} turns this into bloodshed. {quest_time_pressure}: after that, talking won't matter. Both sides need to believe they're being heard.

playerResponses.text: I'll hear both sides. Someone has to before the blades come out.
The workers are right. The elite need to pay what they owe.

dialogueChunks.intro: The landowner took everything. Sent soldiers into the fields at dawn and carted off the entire harvest. Said it was owed in back taxes. Nobody remembers any tax being agreed upon.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} is locked in {quest_origin} under guard. The families who spent six months growing those crops are standing outside with empty hands and empty stomachs. The landowner says the food stays until the 'debt' is paid. The farmers say there is no debt.
dialogueChunks.outro: If someone doesn't {quest_action}, {quest_threat} will set in before winter. The farmers can't fight the guards. They need someone who can change the equation.

playerResponses.text: Theft is theft, even when the thief owns the land. I'll help.
Is there proof the taxes are fabricated?
I'm not getting between a lord and his tenants.

dialogueChunks.intro: Every year, the guild holds examinations to promote apprentices. Every year, the same families pass and the rest fail. The pattern isn't subtle anymore.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} are designed to test knowledge the guild masters only teach to their own relatives. The other apprentices work twice as hard and fail on questions they were never given the material to study. It's a system built to keep power in the same hands, dressed up as meritocracy.
dialogueChunks.outro: One of the failed apprentices found proof: old examination records that show {quest_focus} changing every time an outsider got close to passing. If someone could {quest_action}, the whole rotten system collapses. But the guild has influence everywhere.

playerResponses.text: A rigged game needs someone to flip the table. I'm in.
Exposing corruption is dangerous. What's in it for me?
